  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val Ōēż A Ōł¦ N.val Ōēż B ŌåÆ A = B := by

  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with Ō¤©h_le, h_neqŌ¤®

    simp [h_neq]

      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val Ōēż A.val ŌŖō B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N Ōēż (ŌŖź : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot Ō¢Ė N.prop))

    exact A

variable {M : C Ōźż ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `DŌéü, DŌéé` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `DŌéü` is a refinement of `DŌéé` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `DŌéü` such that `Ōłæ T Ōłł B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (DŌéü DŌéé : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ŌłĆ Ō”āNŌ”ä, N Ōłł DŌéé ŌåÆ Ōłā B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ŌŖå DŌéü Ōł¦ N = sSup B

variable {M : C Ōźż ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N Ōēż A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ŌłĆ N : J, Ōłā A : I, N.val Ōēż A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with Ō¤©NŌéĆ, h_not_leŌ¤®
  have h_ge : NŌéĆ.val ŌŖö sSup I Ōēż sSup J := by
    rw [ŌåÉ sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with Ō¤©h_nŌ¤®
    ┬Ę exact (le_sSup (h_n Ō¢Ė NŌéĆ.prop))
    ┬Ę rename b Ōłł {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) Ōēż sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i Ō¢Ė h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | Ōłā A : I, C Ōēż A.val Ōł¦ C Ōłł J}
  have h_sub : B ŌŖå J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with Ō¤©hŌéü, _Ō¤®
    exact hŌéü.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ┬Ę intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with Ō¤©hŌéü, _Ō¤®
      rcases hŌéü with Ō¤©a, h_a, h_leŌ¤®
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ŌłĆ A : I, Ōłā C ŌŖå B, A Ōēż sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine Ō¤©f A.prop, ?_, by rw [ŌåÉ hf' A.prop]Ō¤®
      intro ╬▒ h_╬▒
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine Ō¤©Ō¤©A, A.prop, ?_Ō¤®, hf A.prop h_╬▒Ō¤®
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_╬▒
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset Ō¤©A, h_A_memŌ¤® with Ō¤©C, h_CŌ¤®
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : NŌéĆ.val Ōłē B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with Ō¤©A, hŌéü, hŌééŌ¤®
    exact (h_not_le (Ō¤©A, hŌéüŌ¤®) hŌéé)
  have h_disj : Disjoint NŌéĆ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' NŌéĆ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : NŌéĆ.val ŌēĀ ŌŖź := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra Ō¢Ė NŌéĆ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < NŌéĆ.val ŌŖö sSup I := by
    rw [ŌåÉ h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (ŌŖż : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ŌŖż := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (ŌŖż : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N Ōēż A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val Ōēż A Ōł¦ N.val Ōēż B ŌåÆ A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with Ō¤©h_le, h_neqŌ¤®
  have h_neq' : A.val ŌēĀ B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s Ōēż I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with Ō¤©h_memŌ¤®
      ┬Ę exact (h_mem Ō¢Ė A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val Ōēż A.val ŌŖō B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N Ōēż (ŌŖź : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot Ō¢Ė N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J ŌåÆ I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' Ō¢Ė N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  ┬Ę simp [RefinementMapId]
  ┬Ę intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec

import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C Ōźż ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ŌŖż
  not_bot_mem' : ŌŖź Ōłē carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' DŌéü DŌéé := by cases DŌéü; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ŌŖż := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ŌŖź Ōłē D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : Ō©å p Ōłł D, p x = ŌŖż := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : Ō©å p Ōłł D, p x = ŌŖż := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I Ōå” p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  ┬Ę sorry
  sorry
  --rw [ŌåÉ iSup_apply, ŌåÉ sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C Ōźż ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `DŌéü, DŌéé` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `DŌéü` is a refinement of `DŌéé` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `DŌéü` such that `Ōłæ T Ōłł B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (DŌéü DŌéé : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ŌłĆ Ō”āNŌ”ä, N Ōłł DŌéé ŌåÆ Ōłā B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ŌŖå DŌéü Ōł¦ N = sSup B

variable {M : C Ōźż ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N Ōēż A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ŌłĆ N : J, Ōłā A : I, N.val Ōēż A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with Ō¤©NŌéĆ, h_not_leŌ¤®
  have h_ge : NŌéĆ.val ŌŖö sSup I Ōēż sSup J := by
    rw [ŌåÉ sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with Ō¤©h_nŌ¤®
    ┬Ę exact (le_sSup (h_n Ō¢Ė NŌéĆ.prop))
    ┬Ę rename b Ōłł {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) Ōēż sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i Ō¢Ė h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | Ōłā A : I, C Ōēż A.val Ōł¦ C Ōłł J}
  have h_sub : B ŌŖå J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with Ō¤©hŌéü, _Ō¤®
    exact hŌéü.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ┬Ę intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with Ō¤©hŌéü, _Ō¤®
      rcases hŌéü with Ō¤©a, h_a, h_leŌ¤®
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ŌłĆ A : I, Ōłā C ŌŖå B, A Ōēż sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine Ō¤©f A.prop, ?_, by rw [ŌåÉ hf' A.prop]Ō¤®
      intro ╬▒ h_╬▒
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine Ō¤©Ō¤©A, A.prop, ?_Ō¤®, hf A.prop h_╬▒Ō¤®
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_╬▒
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset Ō¤©A, h_A_memŌ¤® with Ō¤©C, h_CŌ¤®
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : NŌéĆ.val Ōłē B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with Ō¤©A, hŌéü, hŌééŌ¤®
    exact (h_not_le (Ō¤©A, hŌéüŌ¤®) hŌéé)
  have h_disj : Disjoint NŌéĆ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' NŌéĆ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : NŌéĆ.val ŌēĀ ŌŖź := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra Ō¢Ė NŌéĆ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < NŌéĆ.val ŌŖö sSup I := by
    rw [ŌåÉ h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (ŌŖż : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ŌŖż := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (ŌŖż : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N Ōēż A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val Ōēż A Ōł¦ N.val Ōēż B ŌåÆ A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with Ō¤©h_le, h_neqŌ¤®
  have h_neq' : A.val ŌēĀ B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s Ōēż I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with Ō¤©h_memŌ¤®
      ┬Ę exact (h_mem Ō¢Ė A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val Ōēż A.val ŌŖō B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N Ōēż (ŌŖź : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot Ō¢Ė N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J ŌåÆ I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' Ō¢Ė N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinmentMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ŌłĆ N : J, N.val Ōēż (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  ┬Ę simp [RefinementMapId]
  ┬Ę intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using

h_sub c_a.2)]; apply le_sSup h_ca
          apply le_sSup_of_le c_a.prop h_le
        intro c h_mem_c
        let c' : C := Ō¤©c, h_mem_cŌ¤®
        have h_le_c : c Ōēż sSup (f (h_sub c'.prop)) := by
          rw [ŌåÉ hf' (h_sub c'.prop)]
        apply le_trans h_le_c
        apply sSup_le
        intro a h_mem_a
        have h_a_in_A : a Ōłł A := by
          have h_subs : f (h_sub c'.prop) Ōēż A := by
            apply le_iSup_of_le c'
            exact le_rfl
          exact h_subs h_mem_a
        exact le_sSup h_a_in_A
      rwa [h_aux']

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_J_le_I h_I_le_J
    have h_final_left : ŌłĆ N Ōłł J, N Ōłł I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with Ō¤©h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_IŌ¤®
      let N' : J := Ō¤©N, h_N_in_JŌ¤®
      have h_A : Ōłā A : I, N Ōēż A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with Ō¤©A, h_N_le_AŌ¤®
      choose f hf hf' using h_J_le_I
      let h_BŌéü := hf' N'.prop
      let h_BŌéé := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_BŌéü
      have h_mem : A.val Ōłł f N'.prop := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup (f N'.prop)) := by
          exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_BŌéé h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup (f N'.prop) Ōēż A.val := h_BŌéü Ō¢Ė h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup (f N'.prop) = (ŌŖź : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [ŌåÉ h_BŌéü] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val Ōēż N := by
        simpa [ŌåÉ h_BŌéü] using le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N Ōłł I := by
        exact h_A_eq_N Ō¢Ė A.prop
      aesop
    have h_final_right : ŌłĆ N Ōłł I, N Ōłł J := by
      sorry
    aesop

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.left`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C Ōźż ModuleCat K}

/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]

  coe := carrier

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : Ō©å p Ōłł D, p x = ŌŖż := sorry

open scoped Classical in

    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I Ōå” p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  ┬Ę sorry
  sorry
  --rw [ŌåÉ iSup_apply, ŌåÉ sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C Ōźż ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `DŌéü, DŌéé` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `DŌéü` is a refinement of `DŌéé` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `DŌéü` such that `Ōłæ T Ōłł B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (DŌéü DŌéé : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ŌłĆ Ō”āNŌ”ä, N Ōłł DŌéé ŌåÆ Ōłā B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ŌŖå DŌéü Ōł¦ N = sSup B

variable {M : C Ōźż ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N Ōēż A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ŌłĆ N : J, Ōłā A : I, N.val Ōēż A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with Ō¤©NŌéĆ, h_not_leŌ¤®
  have h_ge : NŌéĆ.val ŌŖö sSup I Ōēż sSup J := by
    rw [ŌåÉ sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with Ō¤©h_nŌ¤®
    ┬Ę exact (le_sSup (h_n Ō¢Ė NŌéĆ.prop))
    ┬Ę rename b Ōłł {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) Ōēż sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i Ō¢Ė h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | Ōłā A : I, C Ōēż A.val Ōł¦ C Ōłł J}
  have h_sub : B ŌŖå J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with Ō¤©hŌéü, _Ō¤®
    exact hŌéü.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ┬Ę intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with Ō¤©hŌéü, _Ō¤®
      rcases hŌéü with Ō¤©A, h_A, h_leŌ¤®
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_A h_le
    have h_le_subset : ŌłĆ A : I, Ōłā C ŌŖå B, A Ōēż sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine Ō¤©f A.prop, ?_, by rw [ŌåÉ hf' A.prop]Ō¤®
      intro ╬▒ h_╬▒
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine Ō¤©Ō¤©A, A.prop, ?_Ō¤®, hf A.prop h_╬▒Ō¤®
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_╬▒
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset Ō¤©A, h_A_memŌ¤® with Ō¤©C, h_CŌ¤®
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : NŌéĆ.val Ōłē B := by
    intro h_contra

      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with Ō¤©A, hŌéü, hŌééŌ¤®
    exact (h_not_le (Ō¤©A, hŌéüŌ¤®) hŌéé)
  have h_disj : Disjoint NŌéĆ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' NŌéĆ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : NŌéĆ.val ŌēĀ ŌŖź := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra Ō¢Ė NŌéĆ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < NŌéĆ.val ŌŖö sSup I := by
    rw [ŌåÉ h_aux]

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N Ōēż A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val Ōēż A Ōł¦ N.val Ōēż B ŌåÆ A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with Ō¤©h_le, h_neqŌ¤®
  have h_neq' : A.val ŌēĀ B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s Ōēż I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with Ō¤©h_memŌ¤®
      ┬Ę exact (h_mem Ō¢Ė A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val Ōēż A.val ŌŖō B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N Ōēż (ŌŖź : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot Ō¢Ė N.prop))

/-- Let `I, J` be direct sum decompositions of `M` with `J` a refinement of `I`.
Then `RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J ŌåÆ I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' Ō¢Ė N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinmentMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ŌłĆ N : J, N.val Ōēż (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  ┬Ę simp [RefinementMapId]
  ┬Ę intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (NŌéü NŌéé : J) (h_leŌéü : NŌéé.val Ōēż RefinementMap I J h NŌéü) :
    RefinementMap I J h NŌéé = RefinementMap I J h NŌéü := by
  have h_leŌéé : NŌéé.val Ōēż (RefinementMap I J h NŌéé) := RefinementMapLE I J h NŌéé
  exact (UniqueGE I J NŌéé (RefinementMap I J h NŌéü) (RefinementMap I J h NŌéé) (Ō¤©h_leŌéü, h_leŌééŌ¤®)).symm

/-- If `N Ōēż A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val Ōēż A ŌåÆ (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (Ō¤©h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h NŌ¤®)]

lemma RefinmentMapFunctorial (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (K : DirectSumDecomposition M) (hij : IsRefinement J I) (hjk : IsRefinement K J)

import PersistentDecomp.Mathlib.Order.Disjoint
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.Indecomposable
import PersistentDecomp.Prereqs.PersistenceSubmodule

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C Ōźż ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ŌŖż
  not_bot_mem' : ŌŖź Ōłē carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' DŌéü DŌéé := by cases DŌéü; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ŌŖż := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ŌŖź Ōłē D := D.not_bot_mem'

lemma pointwise_iSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : Ō©å p Ōłł D, p x = ŌŖż := sorry

lemma pointwise_sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) (x : C) : Ō©å p Ōłł D, p x = ŌŖż := sorry

open scoped Classical in
lemma isInternal (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (c : C) :
    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I Ōå” p.val c := by
  rw [DirectSum.isInternal_submodule_iff_iSupIndep_and_iSup_eq_top]
  constructor
  ┬Ę sorry
  sorry
  --rw [ŌåÉ iSup_apply, ŌåÉ sSup_eq_iSup', I.sSup_eq_top]
  --rfl

variable {M : C Ōźż ModuleCat K} in
/-- Let `DŌéü, DŌéé` be direct sum decompositions of `M` we say that `DŌéü` is a refinement of `DŌéé` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `D`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `DŌéü` such that `Ōłæ T Ōłł B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (DŌéü DŌéé : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=
  ŌłĆ Ō”āNŌ”ä, N Ōłł DŌéé ŌåÆ Ōłā B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M), B ŌŖå DŌéü Ōł¦ N = sSup B

variable {M : C Ōźż ModuleCat K} in
/-- Suppose `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M ` and `J` is a refinement of `I`.
Then for any submodule `N` appearing in `J`, there is a submodule  `A` appearing in `I`
such that `N Ōēż A`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSurj' (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ŌłĆ N : J, Ōłā A : I, N.val Ōēż A.val := by
  by_contra! h_contra
  rcases h_contra with Ō¤©NŌéĆ, h_not_leŌ¤®
  have h_ge : NŌéĆ.val ŌŖö sSup I Ōēż sSup J := by
    rw [ŌåÉ sSup_pair]
    apply sSup_le_iff.mpr
    intro b h_mem
    rcases h_mem with Ō¤©h_nŌ¤®
    ┬Ę exact (le_sSup (h_n Ō¢Ė NŌéĆ.prop))
    ┬Ę rename b Ōłł {sSup (SetLike.coe I)} => h_i
      have h' : sSup (SetLike.coe I) Ōēż sSup J := by
        rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top]
      simp only [Set.mem_singleton_iff] at h_i
      exact (h_i Ō¢Ė h')
  let B : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {C | Ōłā A : I, C Ōēż A.val Ōł¦ C Ōłł J}
  have h_sub : B ŌŖå J := by
    intro C h_C_mem
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_C_mem
    rcases h_C_mem with Ō¤©hŌéü, _Ō¤®
    exact hŌéü.right
  have h_aux : sSup B = sSup I := by
    simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
    constructor
    ┬Ę intro b h_mem
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B] at h_mem
      rcases h_mem with Ō¤©hŌéü, _Ō¤®
      rcases hŌéü with Ō¤©a, h_a, h_leŌ¤®
      exact le_sSup_of_le h_a h_le
    have h_le_subset : ŌłĆ A : I, Ōłā C ŌŖå B, A Ōēż sSup C := by
      intro A
      choose f hf hf' using h
      refine Ō¤©f A.prop, ?_, by rw [ŌåÉ hf' A.prop]Ō¤®
      intro ╬▒ h_╬▒
      simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, B]
      refine Ō¤©Ō¤©A, A.prop, ?_Ō¤®, hf A.prop h_╬▒Ō¤®
      rw [hf' A.prop]
      exact le_sSup h_╬▒
    intro A h_A_mem
    rcases h_le_subset Ō¤©A, h_A_memŌ¤® with Ō¤©C, h_CŌ¤®
    simp only at h_C
    exact le_trans h_C.right (sSup_le_sSup h_C.left)
  have h_aux' : NŌéĆ.val Ōłē B := by
    intro h_contra
    simp only [exists_and_right, Subtype.exists, exists_prop, Set.mem_setOf_eq, SetLike.coe_mem,
      and_true, B] at h_contra
    rcases h_contra with Ō¤©A, hŌéü, hŌééŌ¤®
    exact (h_not_le (Ō¤©A, hŌéüŌ¤®) hŌéé)
  have h_disj : Disjoint NŌéĆ.val (sSup B) := by
    exact (sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup J.sSupIndep' NŌéĆ.prop h_sub h_aux')
  have h_not_bot : NŌéĆ.val ŌēĀ ŌŖź := by
    intro h_contra
    exact J.not_bot_mem (h_contra Ō¢Ė NŌéĆ.prop)
  have h_gt : sSup I < NŌéĆ.val ŌŖö sSup I := by
    rw [ŌåÉ h_aux]
    -- No clue why I couldn't use this theorem from mathlib directly and had to copy paste it here
    -- instead assuming it has to do with needing to bump
    exact (right_lt_sup_of_left_ne_bot h_disj h_not_bot)
  have contra : (ŌŖż : PersistenceSubmodule M) < ŌŖż := by
    rw [I.sSup_eq_top, J.sSup_eq_top] at *
    apply lt_of_lt_of_le h_gt h_ge
  exact (lt_self_iff_false (ŌŖż : PersistenceSubmodule M)).mp contra

/-- Suppose that `I, J` are direct sum decompositions of `M`, `N` is a submodule appearing in `J`
and `A, B` are submodules appearing in `I` such that `N Ōēż A, B`. Then `A = B`. -/
lemma UniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (N : J) (A : I) (B : I) : N.val Ōēż A Ōł¦ N.val Ōēż B ŌåÆ A = B := by
  by_contra h'
  push_neg at h'
  rcases h' with Ō¤©h_le, h_neqŌ¤®
  have h_neq' : A.val ŌēĀ B.val := by
    simp [h_neq]
  have h_disj : Disjoint A.val B.val := by
    let s : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := {A.val, B.val}
    have h_sub : s Ōēż I.carrier := by
      intro X hx
      rcases hx with Ō¤©h_memŌ¤®
      ┬Ę exact (h_mem Ō¢Ė A.prop)
      subst X
      exact B.prop
    exact (sSupIndep_pair h_neq').mp (sSupIndep.mono I.sSupIndep' h_sub)
  have h_le' : N.val Ōēż A.val ŌŖō B.val := le_inf h_le.1 h_le.2
  have h_eq_bot : N Ōēż (ŌŖź : PersistenceSubmodule M) := h_le'.trans <| disjoint_iff_inf_le.mp h_disj
  simp only [le_bot_iff] at h_eq_bot
  exact (J.not_bot_mem' (h_eq_bot Ō¢Ė N.prop))

/-!

# Direct sum decompositions for persistence modules (dual order version)

In this file, we define the type of direct sum decompositions of a persistence module `M`.
This has a natural order given by refinements.

Relation to the material in `PersistentDecomp.DirectSumDecompositionDual.lean`:
here, we are considering the *dual order* of the one constructed in the other file.

-/

open CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits DirectSum
open CompleteLattice hiding sSup_le -- TODO: Fix in mathlib

namespace Dual

variable {C : Type} [Category.{0, 0} C] {K : Type} [DivisionRing K] {M : C Ōźż ModuleCat K}

variable (M) in
/-- A direct sum decomposition of `M ` is a set of non-zero submodules
that are linearly independent and whose supremum is the whole of `M`. -/
@[ext]
structure DirectSumDecomposition where
  carrier : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)
  sSupIndep' : sSupIndep carrier
  sSup_eq_top' : sSup carrier = ŌŖż
  not_bot_mem' : ŌŖź Ōłē carrier

namespace DirectSumDecomposition

instance : SetLike (DirectSumDecomposition M) (PersistenceSubmodule M) where
  coe := carrier
  coe_injective' DŌéü DŌéé := by cases DŌéü; congr!

attribute [-instance] SetLike.instPartialOrder

protected lemma sSupIndep (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSupIndep (SetLike.coe D) := D.sSupIndep'

lemma sSup_eq_top (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : sSup (SetLike.coe D) = ŌŖż := D.sSup_eq_top'
lemma not_bot_mem (D : DirectSumDecomposition M) : ŌŖź Ōłē D := D.not_bot_mem'

    IsInternal (M := M.obj c) (S := Submodule K (M.obj c)) fun p : I Ōå” p.val c := by

/-- Let `DŌéü, DŌéé` be direct sum decomposition of `M` we say that `DŌéü` is a refinement of `DŌéé` if for
every submodule `N` that appears in `DŌéé`, there is a subset `B` of submodules that appear
in `DŌéü` such that `Ōłæ T Ōłł B, T = N`. -/
def IsRefinement (DŌéü DŌéé : DirectSumDecomposition M) : Prop :=

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_J_le_I h_I_le_J
    have h_final_left : ŌłĆ N Ōłł J, N Ōłł I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with Ō¤©h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_IŌ¤®
      let N' : J := Ō¤©N, h_N_in_JŌ¤®
      have h_A : Ōłā A : I, N Ōēż A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with Ō¤©A, h_N_le_AŌ¤®
      choose f hf hf' using h_J_le_I
      let h_BŌéü := hf' N'.prop
      let h_BŌéé := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_BŌéü
      have h_mem : A.val Ōłł f N'.prop := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup (f N'.prop)) := by
          exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_BŌéé h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup (f N'.prop) Ōēż A.val := h_BŌéü Ō¢Ė h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup (f N'.prop) = (ŌŖź : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [ŌåÉ h_BŌéü] at h_last
        subst h_last
        exact (J.not_bot_mem h_N_in_J)
      have h_A_le_N : A.val Ōēż N := by
        simpa [ŌåÉ h_BŌéü] using le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)
      have h_contra : N Ōłł I := by
        exact h_A_eq_N Ō¢Ė A.prop
      aesop
    have h_final_right : ŌłĆ N Ōłł I, N Ōłł J := by

section Indecomposable
variable {D : DirectSumDecomposition M}

/-- If `D` is a direct sum decomposition of `M` and for each `N` appearing in `S` we are given a
direct sum decomposition of `N`, we can construct a refinement of `D`. -/

`RefinementMap` is the function that sends a submodule `N` appearing in `J` to the unique submodule
in `I` that contains `N`. -/
noncomputable def RefinementMap (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : J ŌåÆ I :=
  open scoped Classical in
  if h' : I = J then by
    intro N
    use N
    exact (h' Ō¢Ė N.prop)
  else by
    intro N
    choose A h using (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)
    exact A

@[simp]
lemma RefinementMapId (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (h : IsRefinement I I) (N : I) :
  (RefinementMap I I h) N = N := by
  simp [RefinementMap]

/-- `RefinmentMap N` sends `N` to something it is included in. -/
lemma RefinementMapLE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
  (h : IsRefinement J I) : ŌłĆ N : J, N.val Ōēż (RefinementMap I J h N).val := by
  classical
  obtain rfl | hIJ := eq_or_ne I J
  ┬Ę simp [RefinementMapId]
  ┬Ę intro N
    simpa [RefinementMap, hIJ] using Exists.choose_spec (RefinementMapSurj' I J h N)

/-- Two modules that both decompose the same element are sent to the same submodule by the
`RefinementMap`. -/
lemma RefinementMapSameImage (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (NŌéü NŌéé : J) (h_leŌéü : NŌéé.val Ōēż RefinementMap I J h NŌéü) :
    RefinementMap I J h NŌéé = RefinementMap I J h NŌéü := by
  have h_leŌéé : NŌéé.val Ōēż (RefinementMap I J h NŌéé) := RefinementMapLE I J h NŌéé
  exact (UniqueGE I J NŌéé (RefinementMap I J h NŌéü) (RefinementMap I J h NŌéé) (Ō¤©h_leŌéü, h_leŌééŌ¤®)).symm

/-- If `N Ōēż A` for some `A : I`, then `RefinementMap N = A`. -/
lemma SendsToUniqueGE (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (h : IsRefinement J I) (N : J) (A : I) : N.val Ōēż A ŌåÆ (RefinementMap I J h N) = A := by
  intro h_le
  let B : I := (RefinementMap I J h N)
  rw [UniqueGE I J N A B (Ō¤©h_le, RefinementMapLE I J h NŌ¤®)]

lemma RefinmentMapFunctorial (I : DirectSumDecomposition M) (J : DirectSumDecomposition M)
    (K : DirectSumDecomposition M) (hij : IsRefinement J I) (hjk : IsRefinement K J)
    (hik : IsRefinement K I) (N : K) :
    RefinementMap I K hik N = RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
  have h_le : N.val Ōēż RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N) := by
    apply le_trans (RefinementMapLE J K hjk N)
    exact (RefinementMapLE I J hij _)
  exact SendsToUniqueGE I K hik N (RefinementMap I J hij (RefinementMap J K hjk N)) h_le

/-- The refinement relation defines a preorder on direct sum decompositions of `M` via `DŌéü Ōēż DŌéé` if
and only if `DŌéü` is a refinement of `DŌéé` i.e. this relation is reflexive and transitive. -/
instance : Preorder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le DŌéü DŌéé := IsRefinement DŌéü DŌéé
  --DŌéü Ōēż DŌéé iff DŌéü refines DŌéé.
  le_refl D := by intro N _; use {N}; aesop
  le_trans IŌéü IŌéé IŌéā h12 h23 := by

    rcases (h23 h_mem) with Ō¤©C, h_sub, h_eqŌ¤®
    choose f hf hf' using h12
    let A := Ō©å (c : C), (f (h_sub c.prop))

    constructor
    ┬Ę apply iSup_le_iff.mpr
      intro c

    ┬Ę have h_aux' : sSup A = sSup C := by
        simp_rw [le_antisymm_iff, sSup_le_iff]
        constructor

          have h_aux'' : Ōłā (c : C), a Ōłł (f (h_sub c.prop)) := by aesop

          have h_le : a Ōēż c_a := by rw [hf' (N := c_a) (h_sub c_a.2)]; apply le_sSup h_ca

/-- The refinement relation is antisymmetric. This (combined with the above)
means that it defines a partial order on direct sum decompositions. -/
instance DirectSumDecompLE : PartialOrder (DirectSumDecomposition M) where
  le_antisymm := by
    intro I J h_J_le_I h_I_le_J
    have h_final_left : ŌłĆ N Ōłł J, N Ōłł I := by
      intro N
      by_contra h_neg
      push_neg at h_neg
      rcases h_neg with Ō¤©h_N_in_J, h_N_not_in_IŌ¤®
      let N' : J := Ō¤©N, h_N_in_JŌ¤®
      have h_A : Ōłā A : I, N Ōēż A.val := by
        exact (RefinementMapSurj' I J h_I_le_J) N'
      rcases h_A with Ō¤©A, h_N_le_AŌ¤®
      choose f hf hf' using h_J_le_I
      let h_BŌéü := hf' N'.prop
      let h_BŌéé := hf N'.prop
      simp only at h_BŌéü
      have h_mem : A.val Ōłł f N'.prop := by
        by_contra h_A_not_mem
        have h_aux : Disjoint A.val (sSup (f N'.prop)) := by
          exact (I.sSupIndep.disjoint_sSup A.prop h_BŌéé h_A_not_mem)
        have h_aux' : sSup (f N'.prop) Ōēż A.val := h_BŌéü Ō¢Ė h_N_le_A
        have h_last : sSup (f N'.prop) = (ŌŖź : PersistenceSubmodule M) := by
          rw [disjoint_comm] at h_aux
          exact (Disjoint.eq_bot_of_le h_aux h_aux')
        rw [ŌåÉ h_BŌéü] at h_last
        subst h_last

        simpa [ŌåÉ h_BŌéü] using le_sSup h_mem
      have h_A_eq_N : A.val = N := by
        exact (le_antisymm h_A_le_N h_N_le_A)

        exact h_A_eq_N Ō¢Ė A.prop
      aesop

    aesop
